channel.title: NPR Topics: Religion
channel.description: NPR's stories on U.S. and world religion, spirituality, ethics, and moral issues affecting society and culture. Subscribe to NPR Religion RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran war disrupts Mecca pilgrimage plans for some American Muslims
item.description: Making the pilgrimage to Mecca is a once-in-a-lifetime experience and a sacred obligation for many Muslims. But the ongoing Iran war is making some American Muslims reconsider their plans.
item.content:encoded: Making the pilgrimage to Mecca is a once-in-a-lifetime experience and a sacred obligation for many Muslims. But the ongoing Iran war is making some American Muslims reconsider their plans.
item.dc:creator: Nargis Rahman

item.title: Catholic Diocese fights federal government's effort to take possession of 'holy site'
item.description: The federal government has filed a lawsuit to seize land to build a border wall, sparking a fight over religious freedom. The Catholic Diocese owns the Mount Cristo Rey property it calls a holy site.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has filed a lawsuit to seize land to build a border wall, sparking a fight over religious freedom. The Catholic Diocese owns the Mount Cristo Rey property it calls a holy site.
item.dc:creator: Angela Kocherga

item.title: Israel allows women to take rabbinic test for 1st time, but not become Orthodox rabbis
item.description: After years of court battles, Israel will allow women to take the exams men take to become rabbis. It is a step toward expanding women's leadership roles in Orthodox Judaism.
item.content:encoded: After years of court battles, Israel will allow women to take the exams men take to become rabbis. It is a step toward expanding women's leadership roles in Orthodox Judaism.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: As Hajj nears, Pakistan pilgrims face uncertainty amid regional conflict
item.description: As millions of Muslims prepare for the annual Hajj pilgrimage to Mecca, Pakistan is sending one of the largest groups amid added uncertainty from the war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: As millions of Muslims prepare for the annual Hajj pilgrimage to Mecca, Pakistan is sending one of the largest groups amid added uncertainty from the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: Trump and administration officials address Christian gathering on National Mall
item.description: The conservative prayer event was organized by Freedom 250, a public-private group working with the White House to organize the celebration of America's 250 birthday this summer.
item.content:encoded:
The conservative prayer event was organized by Freedom 250, a public-private group working with the White House to organize the celebration of America's 250 birthday this summer.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Prayer festival calls for more religion in politics, not less
item.description: NPR's Emily Feng speaks with Jack Jenkins of the Religion News Service and with participants and speakers at Rededicate 250, a prayer festival on the National Mall, about the purpose and significance of the event.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Emily Feng speaks with Jack Jenkins of the Religion News Service and with participants and speakers at Rededicate 250, a prayer festival on the National Mall, about the purpose and significance of the event.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: White House organizes a day-long prayer event with private church organizations
item.description: The White House has designated today for a prayer event on the National Mall. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with religious studies scholar Matthew D. Taylor about what's behind "Rededicate 250."
item.content:encoded: The White House has designated today for a prayer event on the National Mall. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with religious studies scholar Matthew D. Taylor about what's behind "Rededicate 250."
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: The White House is planning a big prayer event. Almost all the speakers are Christian
item.description: As the White House prepares for a prayer event tied to the nation's 250th anniversary, some question why the lineup is almost entirely Christian.
item.content:encoded: As the White House prepares for a prayer event tied to the nation's 250th anniversary, some question why the lineup is almost entirely Christian.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: The Trump administration is planning a prayer event on the National Mall. All but one of the speakers is Christian
item.description: The event comes as a new survey finds that many Americans aren't comfortable mixing religion and politics.
item.content:encoded:
The event comes as a new survey finds that many Americans aren't comfortable mixing religion and politics.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Post

item.title: Israel's government is expected to collapse over ultra-Orthodox military draft
item.description: An ultra-Orthodox Jewish party in Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's coalition has called for parliament to be dissolved, threatening to bring down the government.
item.content:encoded:
An ultra-Orthodox Jewish party in Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's coalition has called for parliament to be dissolved, threatening to bring down the government.

(Image credit: Leo Correa)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin
